page_subtitle: Define the structured data you want Ztract to pull from each document.
save_failed: Couldn't save the schema. Please try again.
empty_subtitle: Add your first field to start describing what to extract.
field_name_too_long: Name must be 64 characters or fewer
field_name_duplicate: A sibling field with this name already exists
description_placeholder: Tell the AI what to extract from the document.
description_too_long: Description must be 500 characters or fewer
required_hint: When on, the model must return a value for this field.
enum_options_placeholder: Type an option and press Enter
object_help_body: Group related fields into a single structured object — e.g. an Address (street, city, zip) or Person (first name, last name, DOB).
array_help_body: Use for lists of items where each entry follows the same shape — e.g. line items in an invoice.
init_subtitle: Before you upload documents, tell Ztract what fields you want to pull out of them.
init_method_template_body: Start from a ready-made schema for invoices, receipts, resumes, and more.
init_method_prompt_body: Tell us what to extract — we'll draft a schema you can edit.
init_method_upload_body: We'll analyse one sample file and propose a schema based on it.
init_template_picker_subtitle: Pick the starting point closest to your documents — you can edit fields after.
init_prompt_title: What do you want to extract?
init_prompt_subtitle: Be specific. Example: "Extract invoice number, issue date, supplier, total, and each line item's description, quantity, and unit price."
init_prompt_placeholder: Describe the fields you want Ztract to return from each document…
init_prompt_min_length: Please describe what to extract in at least a sentence.
init_upload_subtitle: Pick one representative file. Ztract will analyse it and draft a schema from its structure.
init_upload_generate_failed: Couldn't build a draft from this file. Please try again.
init_review_subtitle: Edit fields if needed, then save to start extracting.
init_empty_draft: The draft is empty. Start over to pick another method.
init_stub_notice: Using a stub response — backend endpoint is not live yet.
extraction_mode_document_body: Treat the whole file as one extraction unit. Best for single-record documents like invoices or contracts. Non-PDF files (images, Word, Excel, etc.) always behave this way regardless of this setting.
extraction_mode_per_page_body: Run an extraction on every page of each PDF. Best when each page in a multi-page PDF is its own record (statements, batches). This setting only changes behavior for PDFs — other formats remain whole-file.
extraction_mode_change_warning: Saving rebuilds every PDF's extractions in this project and re-queues them. Non-PDF files are unaffected.
extraction_mode_change_confirm_descripti: Switching from "{{from}}" to "{{to}}" deletes every existing extraction and re-queues every document. This will consume page quota again.
sample_mode_title: How should we parse this PDF sample?
sample_mode_description: This PDF has multiple pages. Choose whether the schema should describe the whole document or a single page — the choice also sets the project's extraction mode.
sample_mode_document_body: OCR every page and design fields that match the PDF as one record.
sample_mode_per_page_body: OCR just one page so the AI doesn't average across pages — good when each PDF page is its own record.
